q: Which country has MORE pyramids than Egypt?
ctx: Sudan has over 200 pyramids built by the Kingdom of Kush. They're smaller and pointier than Egypt's, but there are WAY more of them!

q: Where can you find SALTWATER hippos?
ctx: In Guinea-Bissau's Bijagós Islands, hippos swim in the actual ocean. They're the only saltwater hippos in the world!

q: Which country invented the ALPHABET you're reading?
ctx: The Phoenicians of ancient Lebanon created an alphabet around 1050 BC. It evolved into Greek, then Latin, then the ABCs you use today!

q: Where was the Rubik's Cube invented?
ctx: Ernő Rubik invented the cube in 1974 as a teaching tool. It took him a MONTH to solve his own puzzle the first time!

q: Which country has MORE horses than people?
ctx: Mongolia has 13 horses for every person. Kids learn to ride at age 3, and horse racing jockeys are usually 5 to 12 years old!

q: Where is the OLDEST desert on Earth?
ctx: The Namib Desert is 55 million years old. Its sand dunes glow orange at sunrise because the iron in the sand has rusted over millions of years.

q: Which country measures HAPPINESS instead of money?
ctx: Bhutan's Gross National Happiness measures 9 things including health, education, and time spent in nature. GDP isn't even on the list!

q: Where can you RENT the entire country?
ctx: For about $70,000 a night, you can rent Liechtenstein for your event. You get a custom street sign and the key to the country!

q: Which country's flag has an AK-47 on it?
ctx: The AK-47 on Mozambique's flag represents the country's fight for independence. It's the only national flag with a modern firearm.

q: Where do people nod for NO and shake for YES?
ctx: Bulgarians shake their head for 'yes' and nod for 'no.' Visitors get confused every single time!

q: Which country invented BUBBLE TEA?
ctx: Bubble tea was invented in Taichung, Taiwan in the 1980s when someone dropped tapioca balls into iced tea. Now it's a global obsession!

q: Where was COFFEE first traded to the world?
ctx: The port of Mocha in Yemen shipped so much coffee to Europe that 'mocha' became another word for coffee!

q: Which country has 800+ languages?
ctx: With 840 living languages, Papua New Guinea has more linguistic diversity than any country. Some languages have fewer than 100 speakers.

q: Where is the TALLEST waterfall on Earth?
ctx: Angel Falls drops 3,212 feet, so high that the water turns to mist before it reaches the bottom!

q: Which country's flag is NOT a rectangle?
ctx: Nepal's flag has two stacked triangles representing the Himalaya Mountains and the two main religions: Hinduism and Buddhism.

q: Where was the STEEL DRUM invented?
ctx: The steel drum was made from oil barrels in the 1930s. Trinidad literally turned trash into the only acoustic instrument invented in the 20th century!

q: Where do they eat ROLEX for breakfast (not the watch)?
ctx: A Rolex in Uganda is a rolled chapati with an omelet inside. The name comes from 'rolled eggs.' It costs about 50 cents!

q: Which tiny country makes money from .tv domain?
ctx: Tuvalu earns millions each year by licensing its .tv internet domain to television companies around the world!

q: Where was the NECKTIE invented?
ctx: Croatian soldiers wore knotted scarves in the 1600s. The French loved the look, called it 'la cravate' (the Croatian), and the necktie was born!

q: Which country has a Door to Hell burning 50+ years?
ctx: The Darvaza Gas Crater was lit by scientists in 1971, expecting it to burn out in weeks. It's STILL burning over 50 years later!

q: Where is Santa Claus's OFFICIAL home?
ctx: Santa's official post office in Rovaniemi, Finland receives about 500,000 letters from children every year. They answer every single one!

q: Which country has NO ARMY since 1948?
ctx: Costa Rica abolished its army in 1948 and spent the money on schools and healthcare instead. Life expectancy is now higher than the US!

q: Where can you find FANTASY-shaped coffins?
ctx: In Ghana, you can be buried in a coffin shaped like anything: a fish, a car, a shoe, even an airplane!

q: Which country was founded by freed American slaves?
ctx: Liberia's capital Monrovia is named after US President James Monroe. The flag has one star instead of America's 50.

q: Where is the world's LARGEST wine cellar?
ctx: Mileștii Mici wine cellar has underground roads stretching 200 km. You drive through it. There are traffic signs. Underground!

q: Which country hosted the FIRST World Cup?
ctx: The first World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930. Uruguay won it, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final!

q: Where is the LOWEST point on Earth?
ctx: The Dead Sea between Jordan and Israel is 1,412 feet below sea level. You float without trying because the water is 10x saltier than the ocean!

q: Which country has the most CASTLES per person?
ctx: Slovakia has more castles per person than almost any country. Spiš Castle is one of the largest castle complexes in Central Europe!

q: Where is the CLEANEST country in Africa?
ctx: Once a month, ALL Rwandans stop what they're doing and clean together. It's called Umuganda!

q: Which country built SKYSCRAPERS from mud?
ctx: Shibam in Yemen has mud-brick buildings up to 11 stories tall, built in the 1500s. They're called 'the Manhattan of the desert!'

q: Where was LEGO invented?
ctx: Ole Kirk Christiansen named LEGO from 'leg godt,' Danish for 'play well.' LEGO makes more tires per year than any actual tire company!

q: Which country is 93% mountains?
ctx: The Pamir Mountains in Tajikistan are called 'the Roof of the World.' Some villages can only be reached by a single dangerous road.

q: Where is WRESTLING the national sport?
ctx: Senegalese wrestling (laamb) is bigger than soccer there. Wrestlers are national celebrities, and matches fill 30,000-seat stadiums!

q: Which country has a PINK lake?
ctx: Lake Retba is pink because of algae that produce red pigments. The color is most vivid during the dry season!

q: Where is the LARGEST religious building ever made?
ctx: Angkor Wat took about 30 years to build and is the largest religious monument ever constructed. It's on Cambodia's flag!

q: Which country is a CITY and a COUNTRY?
ctx: Singapore is only 31 miles across but has the world's best airport, one of the busiest ports, and some of the best street food anywhere.

q: Where are temples OLDER than the pyramids?
ctx: Malta's Ħaġar Qim temples date to 3600 BC, about 1,000 years older than the Great Pyramid of Giza!

q: Which country has MORE saunas than cars?
ctx: Finland has 3.3 million saunas for 5.5 million people. That's one sauna for every two people. The parliament even has one!

q: Where was SOAP invented?
ctx: Aleppo soap is made from olive oil and laurel oil using a recipe that hasn't changed in 4,000 years. It's the ancestor of all bar soap!

q: Which country invented the word ROBOT?
ctx: The word 'robot' comes from Karel Čapek's 1920 play. In Czech, 'robota' means forced labor. The robots in the play rebel against their creators!

q: Where is the SMALLEST country on Earth?
ctx: Vatican City has about 800 residents, its own post office, radio station, and even a tiny railway station. It's smaller than most golf courses!

q: Which country had an all-female warrior army?
ctx: The Dahomey Amazons fought from the 1600s to 1894. European soldiers called them the most feared warriors they ever faced!

q: Where is the only country in ALL FOUR hemispheres?
ctx: Kiribati straddles the International Date Line AND the equator. It's the only country in all four hemispheres at once!

q: Which country has a Jellyfish Lake?
ctx: Jellyfish Lake's golden jellyfish evolved without predators, so they lost their sting. You can swim through millions of them harmlessly!

q: Where was the World Wide Web invented?
ctx: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in Switzerland in 1989. He gave it away for free instead of patenting it!

q: Which country went from poorest to richest with diamonds?
ctx: In 1966, Botswana was one of the poorest countries on Earth. Then they found diamonds and invested wisely. Now it's one of Africa's richest!

q: Where is the Land of a Million Elephants?
ctx: Laos was called Lan Xang, 'Land of a Million Elephants.' The elephant is still the national symbol, though far fewer remain today.

q: Which Pacific country was NEVER colonized?
ctx: Tonga resisted colonization for centuries. It signed treaties but never gave up sovereignty. The only Pacific kingdom that stayed independent!

q: Where is Africa's biggest film festival?
ctx: FESPACO in Ouagadougou has been running since 1969. It's the Cannes Film Festival of Africa, celebrating African filmmakers!

q: Which country invented FRENCH FRIES (not France!)?
ctx: Belgium claims villagers invented fries by frying potatoes the same way they fried fish. French soldiers tried them and the wrong name stuck!

q: Where is the most DIGITAL country on Earth?
ctx: Estonia lets anyone in the world become a digital citizen through e-Residency. You can start a company in Estonia without ever visiting!

q: Which bird is ON the money AND IS the money?
ctx: The quetzal bird is so sacred in Guatemala that the currency is named after it. In ancient Maya times, its feathers were worth more than gold!

q: Where did the djembe drum come from?
ctx: The djembe drum was originally used by griots (storytellers) to communicate across villages. Now every school music room has one!

q: Which country has TWO capital cities?
ctx: Bolivia has two capitals: Sucre is the constitutional capital and La Paz is the seat of government at the highest altitude in the world!

q: Where is the Pearl of Africa?
ctx: Winston Churchill visited Uganda in 1907 and called it 'the Pearl of Africa' because of its lush green landscapes and perfect climate.

q: Which country invented VOODOO?
ctx: Voodoo originated in Benin and spread to Haiti through the slave trade. In Benin, it's a recognized religion with its own national holiday!

q: Where was the most BOMBED country per person?
ctx: Between 1964 and 1973, the US dropped more bombs on Laos than on all of Europe in WWII. About 30% still haven't exploded.

q: Which country makes most computer chips?
ctx: TSMC in Taiwan makes over 90% of the world's most advanced chips. Almost every phone, laptop, and car depends on them!

q: Where is the TALLEST building on Earth?
ctx: The Burj Khalifa is 2,717 feet tall. People on the top floor see the sunset several minutes AFTER people on the ground!

q: Which country has the LONGEST African coastline?
ctx: Somalia has the longest coastline in Africa at 1,880 miles. Ancient Somalis were master sailors who traded with China, India, and Rome!

q: Where did Indiana Jones explore Petra?
ctx: Petra was carved directly into pink sandstone cliffs 2,000 years ago. It was 'lost' to the Western world until an explorer found it in 1812!

q: Which country produces the most MLB players outside US?
ctx: The Dominican Republic has produced over 800 Major League Baseball players. That's more than any country outside the US!

q: Where was Nikola Tesla born?
ctx: Nikola Tesla invented AC electricity, the technology that powers your house right now. He was born in a village during a lightning storm!

q: Which country invented BUNGEE JUMPING?
ctx: Land diving inspired modern bungee jumping. Men jump from 100-foot towers with vines tied to their ankles as a coming-of-age ritual!

q: Where is the 2nd biggest coral reef?
ctx: The Bay Islands reef is part of the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef, the second largest in the world after Australia's Great Barrier Reef.

q: Which country has 9 sultans who take TURNS being king?
ctx: Every 5 years, a different sultan becomes Malaysia's king. It's like a royal time-share. No other country does this!

q: Where was Game of Thrones filmed in Europe?
ctx: Dubrovnik's old city walls doubled as King's Landing in Game of Thrones. Tourism doubled after the show aired!

q: Which country is the Warm Heart of Africa?
ctx: Lake Malawi has more fish species than any other lake, over 1,000, and scientists keep discovering new ones!

q: Where is the DEEPEST river on Earth?
ctx: The Congo River is up to 720 feet deep in places. It's so deep and fast that fish on opposite banks evolved into different species!

q: Where was Timbuktu's famous university?
ctx: Timbuktu had one of the world's first universities in the 1100s. Its libraries held hundreds of thousands of manuscripts!

q: Which country has 365 beaches?
ctx: Antigua's 365 beaches mean you could visit a different beach every day for a year and never repeat!

q: Where is the Spice Isle?
ctx: Grenada produces so much nutmeg that it's on the national flag. The island smells like nutmeg when you fly over it!

q: Which country has the world's FASTEST human?
ctx: Usain Bolt ran 100 meters in 9.58 seconds. That's about 27 mph. He was so fast he was SLOWING DOWN at the finish line!

q: Where is the NEWEST country on Earth?
ctx: South Sudan became independent on July 9, 2011, making it younger than you!

q: Which country was the first Black republic?
ctx: In 1804, enslaved Haitians defeated Napoleon's army to create the first free Black republic. It inspired freedom movements worldwide!

q: Where are the famous BLUE people?
ctx: The Tuareg people wear indigo-dyed cloth that stains their skin blue over time. That's why they're called the 'Blue People of the Sahara!'

q: Where is the Palace with 1,788 rooms?
ctx: The Sultan's palace has 1,788 rooms, 257 bathrooms, a mosque that holds 1,500 people, and a garage for 7,000 cars!

q: Which country is BASKETBALL's national religion?
ctx: Lithuania is so obsessed with basketball that it's been called a national religion. They've won Olympic bronze three times!

q: Where is the FLATTEST country on Earth?
ctx: The Maldives' highest point is only 5 feet 8 inches above sea level. The entire country could disappear if oceans rise!

q: Which country has a waterfall 4x taller than Niagara?
ctx: Kaieteur Falls drops 741 feet in a single plunge, about 4 times taller than Niagara Falls, into an ancient jungle canyon!

q: Where were Panama hats ACTUALLY invented?
ctx: Panama hats are actually made in Ecuador! They got the wrong name because they were shipped through Panama.

q: Which country produces HALF the world's cork?
ctx: Portugal produces about 100,000 tons of cork per year. Cork oak trees live for 200 years, and the bark regrows after harvesting!

q: Where is the Land of Eagles?
ctx: The eagle on Albania's flag represents freedom and bravery. 'Shqipëria' (Albania in Albanian) means 'Land of Eagles!'

q: Where did chocolate DRINKS originate?
ctx: The ancient Maya heated cacao beans, mixed them with chili and water, and created the first chocolate drinks over 3,000 years ago!

q: Which country is named after the EQUATOR?
ctx: Ecuador literally means 'Equator' in Spanish. The equator runs right through the country. You can stand in both hemispheres at once!

q: Where is the Lion Mountain?
ctx: Sierra Leone means 'Lion Mountain' in Portuguese. One of the largest diamonds ever found, 969 carats, came from here!

q: Which island has Rihanna and rum?
ctx: Rihanna is Barbados's national hero, appointed in 2021 when the country became a republic. Her face is basically everywhere on the island!

q: Where was WINE invented 8,000 years ago?
ctx: Archaeologists found 8,000-year-old clay pots in Georgia with grape residue, the oldest proof of winemaking anywhere on Earth!

q: Which country has the Great Blue Hole?
ctx: The Great Blue Hole is 984 feet across and 410 feet deep. Jacques Cousteau called it one of the top dive sites in the world!

q: Where is the only Dutch-speaking S. American country?
ctx: Suriname is so diverse that a typical neighborhood might celebrate Diwali, Eid, Christmas, and Chinese New Year on the same street!

q: Which country is Africa in miniature?
ctx: Cameroon has beaches, mountains, rainforest, savanna, and desert, which is why it's called 'Africa in miniature!'

q: Where do 30,000 people sing together?
ctx: Latvia's Song Festival happens every 5 years. 30,000 singers perform together in a single choir. You can hear it from miles away!

q: Where do monks collect food EVERY morning?
ctx: Every morning at dawn in Luang Prabang, hundreds of monks walk through town collecting food offerings. It's been happening for centuries!

q: Which country has the Stari Most diving bridge?
ctx: People dive off the Stari Most bridge (79 feet) into the river below. The annual competition has been happening since 1566!

q: Where is Mother Teresa's birthplace?
ctx: Mother Teresa was born in Skopje in 1910 to an Albanian family. She went on to win the Nobel Peace Prize for her work with the poor.

q: Which country is OBSESSED with kite fighting?
ctx: Kite fighting is Afghanistan's national passion. Players coat their strings in ground glass to cut opponents' strings. Last kite flying wins!

q: Where is the Bay of Kotor?
ctx: The Bay of Kotor looks like a fjord but is actually a submerged river canyon. It's one of the most sheltered natural harbors in Europe!

q: Which country's elephants walk through temples?
ctx: In Sri Lanka, elephants are part of religious processions, especially the Kandy Esala Perahera with beautifully decorated elephants!

q: Where is Dracula's Castle?
ctx: Bram Stoker never visited Romania. He based Dracula on stories about Vlad the Impaler, a real prince known for harsh punishments!

q: Which country has the most LIGHTNING?
ctx: Lake Maracaibo gets about 300 lightning storms per year. The 'Catatumbo Lightning' strikes up to 280 times per HOUR!

q: Where is the oldest city (11,000 years)?
ctx: Damascus has been continuously inhabited for over 11,000 years. People have lived there since before humans invented farming!

q: Where are the swimming PIGS?
ctx: The swimming pigs of Big Major Cay paddle out to tourist boats for snacks. Nobody knows how they got to the island in the first place!

q: Which country's CINNAMON flavors the world?
ctx: Sri Lankan cinnamon (Ceylon cinnamon) is the 'true' cinnamon. What most Americans use is actually cassia, a cheaper substitute!

q: Where is the world's biggest SALT FLAT?
ctx: Salar de Uyuni is so flat and reflective that after rain it becomes the world's largest mirror. Satellites use it for calibration!

q: Which country invented GOULASH (by cowboys)?
ctx: Hungarian cowboys (csikós) invented goulash by cooking beef stew in kettles on the plains. Paprika spice is what makes it special!

q: Where did the first country make Bitcoin legal?
ctx: In 2021, El Salvador became the first country to make Bitcoin legal currency. The president announced it at a Bitcoin conference in Miami!

q: Which country gives you PUPUSAS for breakfast?
ctx: Pupusas have been made in El Salvador for over 2,000 years. There's a National Pupusa Day every second Sunday in November!

q: Which country looks like a BOOT on the map?
ctx: Italy's boot shape is one of the most recognizable outlines in the world. The 'ball' it's kicking is the island of Sicily!

q: Where did people first surf on waves?
ctx: Aboriginal Australians were surfing as early as the 1770s when Captain Cook's crew observed them riding waves on wooden boards!

q: Which country's poop is cube-shaped (wombats)?
ctx: Scientists used CT scans to figure out wombat poop is cube-shaped because of how their intestines stretch differently. Science is wild!

q: Where did emojis come from?
ctx: Japanese engineer Shigetaka Kurita created the first 176 emojis in 1999. Now there are over 3,600!

q: Which country has the most PEOPLE on Earth?
ctx: India has 1.4 billion people, more than the entire continent of Africa combined!

q: Where does the Amazon River flow?
ctx: The Amazon River carries more water than the next 7 largest rivers COMBINED. It's so wide in places you can't see the other side!
